factors.name: US Jobs Data (NFP)
factors.summary: Weaker-than-expected nonfarm payrolls reduce Fed rate-hike odds, typically supporting growth/duration equities. TD Securities notes Fed in 'Goldilocks' scenario; gold rallied on this, reducing real-rate pressure on tech valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields / TIPS
factors.summary: Weak jobs data and easing rate-hike expectations compress real yields, which is structurally bullish for long-duration Nasdaq names. UBS sees current yields as buying opportunity in bonds rather than fade.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: Tesla deliveries hit record with European recovery hope (bullish), but Tesla stock sank 7% on report (worst day in ~1 year), signaling sentiment fragility. Meta AI agent progress slower than expected per Zuckerberg (bearish for AI narrative). Nvidia offers compute swaps for startups (neutral—monetization flexibility).

factors.name: AI Sector Momentum
factors.summary: Meta's AI agents underperforming vs OpenAI expectations, and Cointelegraph noted 'AI sector weakness' alongside recent crypto/risk-off moves. Two-day tech-led selloff cited as concern that AI rally ran ahead of fundamentals.

factors.name: Equities Sector Breadth
factors.summary: Nasdaq closed -0.80% (down 207 points) yesterday despite Dow up 1.14%. Tech underperformance vs cyclicals suggests rotation out of growth; S&P 500 flat signals consolidation, not conviction.

factors.name: China Economic Data
factors.summary: China services PMI eased to 54.1 (vs 54.4 prior) but beat estimates; Vietnam growth jumped to 8.39% and Q2 trade beat. Mixed signals—China resilient but moderating. PBOC set yuan at 6.8047 (weaker vs 6.7830 prior close), potential weakness headwind for US exporters.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD weakened sharply on soft NFP; GBP/USD near 1.3350, AUD/USD positive for second straight day, NZD/USD toward 0.5700. Weaker dollar structurally supports Nasdaq's large international revenue base.

factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: UBS and TD Securities both signal Fed on hold or delayed tightening. Warsh quoted as downplaying inflation risk despite hike bets, contradicting hard-line Trump pressure. Rate-cut bets now favored into end-2026.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield
factors.summary: No explicit yield data in news feed, but soft jobs + dovish Fed repricing should push yields lower intraday. Long-duration growth names (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) benefit from yield compression.

factors.name: Geopolitical / Energy
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz flows resume post-Iran ceasefire; WTI ~$68.40 (easing). Supply normalization removes inflation/growth shock risk. Oil down ~2% on month; less tail risk for Fed tightening.

factors.name: Holiday Liquidity / Market Microstructure
factors.summary: US markets closed Friday (Independence Day). Thin holiday trading Thursday drove mixed close; elevated volatility risk on thin flows. Friday close will be very light; gaps/squeezes possible Monday reopening.

factors.name: Private Credit / Credit Stress
factors.summary: Blue Owl and other private credit managers capping redemptions (2nd quarter in a row); $15.6B redemption requests vs $5.9B payouts. Liquidity crunch signals risk-off tail; potential contagion if spreads blow out.

factors.name: Antitrust / Regulation
factors.summary: Supreme Court blocked effort to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook; Trump allies renewing push to reshape Fed. No direct antitrust news on mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META), but regulatory overhang persists.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in feed, but tech selloff + thin holiday liquidity suggest elevated vol regime. Gamma risk on mega-cap calls likely in play given recent swings.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin held $61K after weak jobs data; analysts divided on next move. BTC-Nasdaq correlation still positive (risk-on), but no directional conviction in crypto flow yet.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is caught in a two-way tug: soft US jobs data (NFP miss) and dovish Fed repricing support duration/growth equities via lower real yields and reduced rate-hike odds. However, the selloff in mega-cap tech (Tesla -7%, Meta AI concerns) and the Nasdaq's -0.80% close Thursday (vs Dow +1.14%) signal investors are rotating into value/cyclicals and doubting AI upside. Private credit stress and holiday-thinned liquidity are additional headwinds. The 4h gain of +0.44% overnight is minor and within noise. Overall sentiment is fragile—macro tailwinds (softer rates, weaker dollar) clash with micro headwinds (execution doubts, credit cracks, sector rotation).
catalysts_ahead: US markets CLOSED Friday (July 4 Independence Day) — next session Monday July 7
No major US economic releases expected Friday; thin holiday trading
Earnings season wrapping: watch for any pre-market guidance revisions over weekend
FOMC meeting and CPI release next week (early July) — high bar for conviction until clarity
Geopolitical: Iran ceasefire negotiations ongoing; any escalation re-prices energy, growth risk
Private credit redemptions tracking into Q3 — monitor Blue Owl, Ares, Apollo for gating announcements
screening.risk_warning: Bullish bias invalidated if: (1) jobless claims reaccelerate or unemployment jumps next week, forcing Fed re-hawking; (2) CPI surprise hot, crushing real-yield bullish case; (3) tech earnings next week disappoint or guide down on AI spending; (4) private credit funds gate more aggressively, triggering contagion sell-off; (5) China data rolls over further, reviving global recession fears. Bearish bias invalidated if: (1) jobs data is retested stronger; (2) Fed cuts sooner/larger than expected; (3) mega-cap earnings smash expectations with robust forward guidance; (4) Strait of Hormuz closes again, oil spikes, and inflation re-accelerates.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2; overnight +0.44% bounce is shallow. Wait for retest of 28900 support with confirmed hold + rejection of lower prices before committing longs. For shorts, resistance at 29600–30000 is where profit-taking becomes material; no conviction on extended downside until breadth and credit conditions clarify post-Friday close and next week's data.

factors.name: US NFP Report (June)
factors.summary: June non-farm payrolls came in at +57K vs +110K expected, significantly undershooting consensus. This has triggered dovish re-pricing of Fed expectations and eased rate-hike bets, reducing downside risk for long-duration equities but also confirming growth concerns.

factors.name: Fed Rate-Hike Bets Easing
factors.summary: Weak NFP has compressed Fed tightening expectations; UBS and other strategists now see elevated yields as attractive entry points and reduced inflation pressure from labor market. Lower real yields support growth multiples.

factors.name: AI Sector Momentum & Valuation Concerns
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'AI Trade Is Losing One of Its Key Signals' with pricing power drifting lower in semiconductor/cloud sectors. Zuckerberg notes AI agent development hasn't accelerated as expected. Invesco's Fiona Yang warns parts of the market have become 'stretched.' This signals peak enthusiasm and potential multiple compression.

factors.name: Tech Earnings Outlook & Forward Guidance Uncertainty
factors.summary: H1 2026 saw 'bumper earnings' and AI enthusiasm, but market focus now shifts to sustainability of the AI buildout and second-half capex/margin visibility. No major earnings or guidance surprises logged in this news window; uncertainty elevated.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: USD Index near 100.80, declining for second consecutive day after weak NFP; weak dollar typically supports US exporters and Nasdaq large-caps with international revenue exposure.

factors.name: China Economic Resilience
factors.summary: China Services PMI eased slightly to 54.1 (June) from 54.4, but remains in expansion; export orders hit 20-month high, signalling external rebalancing and pricing power recovery. Vietnam growth also beat estimates. Supports demand for US semiconductor/tech exports.

factors.name: Market Sentiment & Risk-Off Rotation
factors.summary: South Korean stocks initially plunged 10% over two sessions before rebounding 5% on Friday, reflecting 'skittish investor sentiment' and 'scrutiny of AI boom sustainability.' Private credit redemption requests far exceed payouts ($15.6B requested vs $5.9B paid), signalling liquidity stress and potential risk-off contagion.

factors.name: US Holiday Weekend & Thin Liquidity
factors.summary: US markets closed Friday (July 4 weekend) for Independence Day; Japanese authorities signalling intervention readiness; options traders hedging yen volatility. Thin liquidity and geopolitical risks (Iran, Hormuz) may amplify moves but dampen signal clarity.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield & Real Rates
factors.summary: No specific 10Y yield data in news, but dovish re-pricing post-NFP should support long-duration growth equities. Real yield direction not explicitly stated; hard to assess without TIPS curves.

factors.name: 24h Price Action: -1.24% Decline
factors.summary: NAS100 down 1.24% over 24h but only +0.44% in 4h, suggesting overnight weakness from yesterday's selloff (Nasdaq -0.8% on rotation into value after weak NFP) is stabilizing. Move extended downward; late-stage retracement setup.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq rallied hard on H1 2026 AI enthusiasm and strong earnings, but sentiment has shifted sharply this week. Weak US NFP (+57K vs +110K) confirmed growth concerns and triggered a rotation from mega-cap tech into value/Dow, with Nasdaq falling 0.8% yesterday. However, dovish Fed re-pricing (lower rate-hike odds) and resilient China/Vietnam data provide technical support for long-duration growth multiples. The core tension: AI capex may not deliver ROI as expected (Zuckerberg, AI pricing power erosion), yet lower rates reduce cost-of-capital headwinds. Market is oscillating between 'growth scare = buy the dip on rate cuts' and 'AI capex disappointment = fundamentals challenged.' Friday's +0.44% in 4h suggests stabilization after -1.24% 24h decline, but thin holiday liquidity and extended downside phase make high-conviction calls risky.
catalysts_ahead: Weekly PMI data (Eurozone, UK) — Friday European open; muted impact per Bloomberg unless major divergence
Next week: Potential FOMC speaker commentary on Fed path post-NFP
Upcoming mega-cap earnings: Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia, Google guidance on cloud capex and AI ROI expectations — critical for NAS100 repricing
CPI/PCE inflation data early July — will anchor real yield re-pricing
China trade/manufacturing data — support thesis for sustained capex demand
US corporate earnings guidance — any downward revisions on growth/margin outlooks would trigger renewed selloff
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Mega-cap tech earnings/guidance materialize strong (beats, bullish 2H capex/margin forecasts) → sharp reversal upward; (2) Fed speakers turn hawkish despite weak NFP, re-pricing rate hikes → real yields spike, long-duration growth crushed; (3) China reopens aggressive stimulus or US sees surprise economic rebound → growth narrative re-ignites, AI enthusiasm resurges; (4) Volatility spike on geopolitical shock (Iran/Hormuz) forces risk-off, even if dovish-supportive for growth equities, liquidity evaporates and technicals break down.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended downward (down 1.24% in 24h, now stabilizing +0.44% in 4h). Do not chase short here; wait for pullback retest of 29,600–29,800 resistance with declining volume/momentum, then reassess bias on fresh NFP/PMI/earnings data. If 29,100 support breaks decisively on below-trend volume, 28,800 is next structural target; only then consider stronger bearish positioning. Otherwise, hold NEUTRAL and let market clarify via upcoming catalysts.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Repricing (Dovish)
factors.summary: US NFP came in at +57K vs +110K expected (per Investinglive), triggering sharp dovish repricing. July hike odds fell to 17% from 30%, September hikes now ~50%. Lower rate expectations typically support long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: Dollar weakened broadly post-NFP and suspected BoJ intervention on USD/JPY. Weaker USD is a tailwind for US multinational earnings (heavy NQ exposure) and competitive positioning for US tech exports.

factors.name: Real Yields Compression
factors.summary: Softer labour data and reduced Fed tightening bets push nominal yields lower. Real yields (TIPS) likely declined, reducing the hurdle rate for high-multiple growth/AI names dominant in Nasdaq 100.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Direction
factors.summary: No explicit 10Y level cited, but dovish repricing implies yields traded lower overnight into US session open. Long-duration mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, TSLA) benefit materially from lower nominal yields.

factors.name: Tech ETF Flows & Sentiment
factors.summary: BofA reports largest US stock outflows in 3+ months (per Bloomberg), suggesting investor caution. However, outflows are from broad equity, not tech-specific reversal; mixed signal.

factors.name: Nasdaq Futures Overnight
factors.summary: NQ futures advanced 1.0% to ~29,850 during European hours (per FXStreet 07:21 UTC), well above current spot 29,329, indicating momentum into US cash open (though markets are closed for July 4).

factors.name: AI/Hyperscaler Capex Outlook
factors.summary: No major cloud capex guidance or semiconductor demand updates in news window. Tesla deliveries hit record and European recovery noted (bullish for TSLA), but no broad AI/chip catalyst confirmed.

factors.name: Mega-cap Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No imminent earnings surprises or guidance updates mentioned. Next cycle earnings risk sits ahead; current move driven purely by macro (Fed repricing), not fundamentals.

factors.name: Sector Rotation (Value vs Growth)
factors.summary: Yesterday's close showed Dow +1.1%, Nasdaq -0.8%, indicating rotation into value. Today's 4h move (+0.44%) shows modest Nasdaq recovery, but intraday chop suggests conviction still building.

factors.name: China/Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire news and Strait of Hormuz chatter reduce tail risk slightly. Venezuela earthquake (non-market-moving for tech). No Taiwan tensions escalation noted.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq 100 is in early Phase 2 of a dovish repricing cycle triggered by weaker-than-expected US NFP data (+57K vs +110K) on July 2. Fed rate hike expectations collapsed (July: 17% vs 30% prior; September: ~50%), driving multi-asset repricing: USD weakness, Treasury yields lower, real yields compressed. This is a direct tailwind for long-duration growth/AI mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA, GOOGL, META) which dominate the NDX. Overnight NQ futures rallied 1.0% to ~29,850, but current spot is 29,329 (+0.44% in 4h, -1.24% in 24h), suggesting the move is partially in but not extended. However, the 24h YTD weakness and fresh bullish momentum from macro repricing indicate Phase 2 entry conditions. US markets are closed today (July 4 prep), which may limit follow-through conviction.
catalysts_ahead: US market reopens Monday (July 7): Risk of weekend volatility repricing or chop; follow-through on dovish repricing agenda critical
Fed minutes (date TBD, likely within week): Could clarify Fed's path and resolve current 'two hikes vs hold' ambiguity
Mega-cap earnings cycle (late July–early August): AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA guidance will test whether macro repricing is warranted
Next CPI/PCE release (mid-July): Inflation print could re-anchor Fed expectations; hotter-than-expected data would snap dovish narrative
Treasury auctions and 10Y re-equilibration: Yields may stabilize or rebound if real yield overshoots lower
screening.risk_warning: Move extended too quickly if NQ rallies past 30,000 without 72h consolidation — late-phase chasing will be punished on retest. Key invalidations: (1) CPI surprise hotter than expected → Fed repricing reverses, yields snap higher, growth multiples compress; (2) Mega-cap earnings guide down on slowing demand → tech cuts forward guidance despite lower rates; (3) Real yields stabilize/rise on supply concerns → long-duration names lose tailwind; (4) US market holiday liquidity + thin positioning creates whipsaw risk — avoid aggressive size into US open until breadth confirmed.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is partially underway in futures; wait for Monday US cash open retest of 29,500–29,650 support/pivot before adding long positions. If NQ reclaims 29,850+ on volume with 10Y below 3.95%, escalate. Avoid chasing into resistance 30,000 without fresh catalyst (earnings, Fed clarity).

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Dovish Repricing
factors.summary: US NFP report triggered sharp repricing lower in Fed hike odds (30 bps by year-end vs prior 40+ bps; July hike odds down to 17% from 30%). Softer jobs data and easing hawkish Fed prospects are lifting long-duration growth equities, per Deutsche Bank and multiple FX strategists.

factors.name: Real Yields Lower, Duration Tailwind
factors.summary: Weaker USD and fading inflation concerns reduce real yield pressures. Gold rallying to $4,100+/oz (breaking 4-week losing streak) and silver +2.72% confirm risk-on pivot and reduced real rate risk—tech mega-caps benefit directly from lower TIPS yields.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD/JPY fell to 160.60 from 162.50, GBP up 1.3% weekly (strongest in 3mo), EUR/USD near 1.1455. Weaker dollar reduces headwinds for Nasdaq's ~40% international revenue exposure, historically a tailwind in risk-on cycles.

factors.name: Crypto Risk-On Signal
factors.summary: Bitcoin ETFs posted $221.7M inflows (strongest since early May), snapping 10-day losing streak; BTC bounced above $61k. Historically strong NQ-BTC linkage suggests risk appetite is returning and sentiment may be bottoming.

factors.name: AI/Semiconductor Momentum Mixed
factors.summary: Micron in focus (Michael Burry disclosed short, CEO quality push at Ford). Memory/semiconductor stocks lost momentum intraday per CoinDesk, but AI token prices remain tracked as traders assess capex cycle. No major earnings or hyperscaler guidance since earnings window closed.

factors.name: Equity Flow Weakness
factors.summary: BofA reports largest US stock outflows in >3 months, indicating investor rotation away from equities despite dovish repricing. Suggests contrarian headwind to upside conviction even as rate narrative improves.

factors.name: Europe Services PMI Soft (Partial Offset)
factors.summary: UK services PMI 48.8 (contraction, steepest since late 2022), Eurozone composite 50.0, France 47.2—all near or in contraction. EU weakness limits synchronized global risk-on narrative and could weigh on tech demand outlook.

factors.name: Trump Fed/Policy Noise
factors.summary: Trump allies pushing to reshape Fed and Trump doubling down on Fed changes post-Supreme Court blocking of Lisa Cook removal. Market has largely priced this in; not a near-term earnings driver but adds tail-risk noise.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in early-stage risk-on re-entry driven by softer-than-expected US NFP Friday and sharp dovish repricing: Fed hike odds collapsed to 30 bps by year-end, real yields are falling, USD weakness is emerging, and crypto inflows confirm sentiment rebound. However, the move (+0.44% in 4h, -1.24% in 24h) is already 30-60 min into its initial phase, and large concurrent US equity outflows (BofA data) suggest institutional conviction is lagging retail/algorithmic flows. European PMI weakness and Micron short disclosure add headwinds. This is a classic post-catalyst bounce into a potential pullback zone—not yet extended, but no longer in pure PHASE 1 conviction territory.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Minutes (likely early next week)—will clarify Committee debate on further tightening; market pricing in minimal hikes, so dovish tone could accelerate NQ rally
Weekly jobless claims (next Thursday)—any further labor softness re-enforces dovish Fed narrative
CPI, Core CPI (mid-month)—inflation data surprise risk remains; hot print would reverse today's repricing instantly
NVDA earnings window / hyperscaler capex guidance—semiconductor demand thesis still live but no imminent catalysts visible in ~72h window
Tech mega-cap earnings season tail-end—AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL guidance could reset growth expectations
screening.risk_warning: Largest risk: equity outflows persist despite rate rally, invalidating the assumption that dovish repricing = automatic equity re-rating. If CPI surprises hot next week, entire narrative flips to bearish. Europe PMI contraction deepens, dragging tech hardware/software demand expectations lower. Micron short disclosure and AI capex cycle questions could spark selloff in semis/cloud if guidance disappoints. Real yields could spike again if safe-haven bid reverses (geopolitical flare-up, risk-off). Move is only +0.44% in 4h and -1.24% net 24h, so NAS100 is still technically close to entry levels but psychological momentum is shifting—wait for clean retest of 29,200-29,300 support before full conviction.
screening.entry_guidance: Buy-on-dip into 29,200–29,350 zone on pullback after initial move thins out; avoid chasing at or above 29,500 resistance in absence of fresh catalyst. If move extends >1% higher without pullback, hold long exposure but reduce new entries until FOMC/CPI calendar clarifies inflation/rate risk.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: US NFP released Thursday showed weakness, dropping July hike probability from 30% to 17% and setting up dovish repricing. This reduces headwinds for long-duration growth assets like tech megacaps. FXStreet, TD Securities, and MUFG all note shift toward rate-cut risk by year-end.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: Softer labor data + fading Fed hike expectations have compressed nominal yields and supported real yields downward, reducing discount rates on high-multiple Nasdaq constituents. Gold rallying to $4147 and breaking 5-week losing streak confirms risk-off/lower-real-yield regime.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD Index set for negative weekly close after weak NFP. Weaker dollar supports international revenue of Nasdaq heavyweights (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL) and reduces FX headwinds. BoJ suspected stealth intervention supports JPY, adding to dollar weakness narrative.

factors.name: Tech/AI Sentiment
factors.summary: Mixed signals: Tesla robotaxi launch in Miami is positive; however, MacroResearchBoard warns of 'looming AI disappointment' and rising yields. Michael Burry reported shorting Micron, signaling bearish AI sentiment among contrarians. Memory/semiconductor stocks lost momentum Friday per CoinDesk report.

factors.name: Equity Flows & Risk-On
factors.summary: US equity funds drew inflows as tech buying resumed (Stock Market News). European shares cruised to record highs on cool US data and geopolitical progress. Crypto also stabilized with Bitcoin ETF inflows of $222M, snapping 10-day losing streak—risk-on mood intact.

factors.name: Macro Divergence Risk
factors.summary: Fed and ECB seen diverging: Fed likely holding while ECB remains hawkish due to energy/geopolitical risks (per French chief economists). This could limit upside if growth concerns override rate-cut optimism. General Mills reported consumer stress despite pet spending strength.

factors.name: Geopolitical / Energy
factors.summary: Iran funeral ceremonies, NATO tensions, US-Iran peace accord reducing Persian Gulf tensions. OPEC output surged in June; LME Aluminium unwound geopolitical risk premium. Broadly constructive but event-dependent. Israel stocks down 0.44%, minor headwind.

factors.name: Price Action / Momentum
factors.summary: NDX +0.44% in 4h but -1.24% over 24h—modest strength into US holiday but yesterday's weakness lingers. Bias history shows two consecutive BUY reads (6/10 confidence) with 'no confirmation triggered,' indicating move not yet established.

key_levels.support: 29150–29200 (prior 4h lows; macro support if NFP-driven bounce rolls over)
key_levels.resistance: 29400–29500 (Friday session highs; initial target on dovish follow-through)

screening.narrative: Fresh dovish repricing from weak US NFP (Thursday) has sparked a broad shift in Fed rate-hike expectations (17% vs 30% pre-release) and triggered inflows into equities, particularly tech, as long-duration growth valuations expand on lower real yields. Dollar weakness and risk-on sentiment (equity flows, Bitcoin stabilization, European record highs) support the bounce, but confidence is capped by (a) move already in progress over past 24–36h, (b) conflicting commentary on AI disappointment and consumer stress, and (c) diverging Fed/ECB policy reducing margin of safety. Nasdaq at +0.44% 4h is extending the dovish move, not initiating it.
catalysts_ahead: US Services ISM (June, Friday late or weekend) — likely to confirm broad-based slowdown, reinforcing dovish narrative
ISM Manufacturing final (if released)
Weekly jobless claims (Thursday next week) — labor market confirmation
Fed speakers (Barkin, Bowman, etc.) over next 72h — could push back on rate-cut narrative
FOMC meeting (mid-July) — critical for medium-term bias; dovish hold vs hawkish hold
Earnings season: Tesla, Nvidia guidance expected to dominate AI/capex narrative
screening.risk_warning: If Services ISM rebounds sharply or Fed speakers pushback against dovish repricing (re-hawking), this move extends into resistance and confidence collapses. Additionally, if PCE or CPI surprise hot in next 2 weeks, real yields could spike and crush tech multiples. Macro slowdown + consumer stress (General Mills commentary) could also trigger risk-off flight, especially if equity inflows reverse. Watch VXN and QQQ sentiment for signs of exhaustion.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already in progress (phase 2); wait for a pullback to 29200–29250 support before adding longs. If break above 29450 resistance on fresh dovish catalyst (Services ISM), consider trending entry. Avoid chasing into US holiday thinness (low liquidity, whipsaw risk).
